is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria cases treated with CQ, SP or AQ.

reports_target_markers.evidence: PCR genotyping used only msp-1 and msp-2 loci to distinguish recrudescence from re-infection; no molecular drug-resistance marker codons (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, etc.) were genotyped or reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Only in vivo clinical efficacy failure proportions are reported (Table 4); no per-locus molecular marker frequencies.

sub_country_location.evidence: Data reported by site: Freetown, Kabala, Kailahun, Makeni, Matru (Table 4).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected between October 2002 and May 2003, within a 3-year window.

african_field_samples.evidence: Clinical isolates collected from children at hospital/clinic sites across Sierra Leone.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary in vivo efficacy study at five sites.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Part of the Sierra Leone Antimalarial Efficacy Study Collaboration, but this is a specific new field collection reported here.

supplement.evidence: No molecular marker data mentioned or pointed to in supplementary materials; study did not genotype resistance markers.

assessment.summary: An in vivo therapeutic efficacy study of chloroquine, sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine at five sites in Sierra Leone (2002-2003). PCR genotyping used only msp-1 and msp-2 for recrudescence/re-infection distinction. No drug-resistance molecular markers (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1) were genotyped, so no extractable per-locus marker frequencies exist.
exclusion_reasons: No target drug-resistance marker codons genotyped or reported (only clinical efficacy and msp-1/msp-2 typing)
No extractable per-locus molecular marker frequencies
